followupAnswerBank.question: How did you handle the parent-child relationship in RAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: RAP mein, parent-child relationship CDS associations aur BDEF compositions use karke model hoti hai. Parent CDS view child entity ke liye ek association define karta hai. Parent BDEF child ke saath composition relationship declare karta hai, deep create aur update enable karte hue. Jab user ek Fiori transaction mein line items ke saath purchase request banata hai, framework header aur items dono ko atomically banane handle karta hai. Child BDEF parent ko 'association to parent ZI_PurchaseRequest as _PurchReq' ke through reference karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In RAP, parent-child relationship is modeled using CDS associations and BDEF compositions. The parent CDS view defines an association to the child entity. The parent BDEF declares a composition relationship to the child, enabling deep create and update. When a user creates a purchase request with line items in one Fiori transaction, the framework handles creating both the header and items atomically. The child BDEF references the parent via 'association to parent ZI_PurchaseRequest as _PurchReq'.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Purchase request had header and line items. CDS: ZI_PurchaseRequest with association to ZI_PurchaseItem. BDEF parent: composition [0..*] of ZI_PurchaseItem as _Items. BDEF child: association to parent ZI_PurchaseRequest as _PurchReq. Fiori object page automatically showed line items table in a section — pure annotation-driven, no custom code.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Child BDEF mein 'association to parent' declare karna bhoolna — framework cannot navigate parent-child and deep operations fail. Aur child entity ke liye separate lock master declare karna — child should use 'lock dependent by _PurchReq' to share parent lock.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you implement the approve and reject actions?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Approve aur reject actions behavior implementation mein FOR ACTION methods ke roop mein implemented the. Har method current status ko READ ENTITIES use karke padhta hai double-processing se guard karne ke liye — agar already approved hai, error return hota hai. Fir MODIFY ENTITIES Status field ko APPROVED ya REJECTED pe update karta hai aur ApprovedBy field ko current user pe set karta hai. Result parameter updated entity return karta hai taaki Fiori object page ko automatically refresh kare. Fiori @UI.lineItem aur @UI.identification annotations control karte hain action buttons kahan appear hote hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The approve and reject actions were implemented as FOR ACTION methods in the behavior implementation. Each method reads the current status using READ ENTITIES to guard against double-processing — if already approved, an error is returned. Then MODIFY ENTITIES updates the Status field to APPROVED or REJECTED and sets the ApprovedBy field to the current user. The result parameter returns the updated entity so Fiori refreshes the object page automatically. Fiori @UI.lineItem and @UI.identification annotations control where the action buttons appear.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Edge case: manager tried to approve an already-rejected request. Without status guard, it would silently re-approve. Added status check in FOR ACTION — if Status = 'REJECTED' then append error to REPORTED and FAILED. Business loved this guard — it prevented accidental re-processing. This kind of defensive coding shows real project maturity.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Action mein result parameter populate karna bhoolna — et_result return karna zaroori hai else Fiori object page refresh nahi hoga after action. Aur action BDEF mein declare karna lekin @UI annotation add karna bhoolna — button Fiori pe nahi dikhega.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
METHOD approve FOR ACTION
    IMPORTING keys FOR PurchReq~approve RESULT et_result.
  READ ENTITIES OF ZI_PurchaseRequest3
    ENTITY PurchReq FIELDS ( Status )
    WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys ) RESULT DATA(lt_req).
  LOOP AT lt_req INTO DATA(ls).
    IF ls-Status = 'APPROVED' OR ls-Status = 'REJECTED'.
      APPEND VALUE #( %key = ls-%key ) TO failed-purchreq.
      APPEND VALUE #( %key = ls-%key
        %msg = new_message_with_text(
          severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
          text = 'Request already processed' ) )
        TO reported-purchreq.
    ELSE.
      MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_PurchaseRequest3
        ENTITY PurchReq UPDATE FIELDS ( Status ApprovedBy )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %key = ls-%key
          Status = 'APPROVED' ApprovedBy = sy-uname ) ).
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
  et_result = VALUE #( FOR r IN lt_req ( %key = r-%key %param = r ) ).
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What was your testing strategy for the RAP app?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Testing teen levels pe ki gayi thi. RAP test doubles ke liye CL_ABAP_BEHV_TESTDOUBLE ke saath ABAP Unit use karke unit tests — entity data mock karke validation logic ko real database records ki zarurat ke bina isolation mein test karna. ADT Fiori Elements preview ke through integration testing — saari screens, form flows, validation error display, aur action buttons test karna. Different roles ke real users ke saath user acceptance testing — requesters, managers, aur finance — authorization, workflow, aur end-to-end business flow verify karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing was done at three levels. Unit tests using ABAP Unit with CL_BOTD_BEHV_TEST_ENVIRONMENT for RAP test doubles — mocking the entity data to test validation logic in isolation without needing real database records. Integration testing via the ADT Fiori Elements preview — testing all screens, form flows, validation error display, and action buttons. User acceptance testing with real users in different roles — requesters, managers, and finance — to verify authorization, workflow, and end-to-end business flow.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Unit test ne ek edge case pakad liya: negative amount ke liye validation check missing tha. Budget check sirf 'amount > limit' check karta tha, lekin amount < 0 allowed tha. Unit test add kiya for negative amount — validation failed — budget check extended to also reject negative. UAT mein yeh issue nahi aaya kyunki unit test ne pehle pakad liya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: RAP testing mein sirf manual testing karna — unit tests skip karna. ABAP Unit CL_BOTD_BEHV_TEST_ENVIRONMENT se validation logic isolate karke test karna best practice hai. Yeh regression catch karta hai future changes mein.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ABAP Unit test for validateBudget
CLASS ltc_validate_budget DEFINITION FOR TESTING
    DURATION SHORT RISK LEVEL HARMLESS.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS test_amount_exceeds_budget FOR TESTING.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS ltc_validate_budget IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD test_amount_exceeds_budget.
    " Use RAP test double framework
    DATA lo_test TYPE REF TO cl_botd_behv_test_environment.
    " Setup mock entity with amount > budget
    " Call validation method
    " Assert: failed-purchreq has 1 entry
    " Assert: reported-purchreq has error message
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial(
      act = failed-purchreq
      msg = 'Expected validation failure for amount > budget' ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you transport the RAP objects to production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Saare RAP artifacts ek single transport request mein together transport hote hain — database table, draft table, CDS view entity, behavior definition, behavior implementation class, service definition, aur service binding. Transport import sequence matter karta hai — database table CDS view activate hone se pehle exist hona chahiye, aur CDS view BDEF activate hone se pehle exist hona chahiye. Eclipse ADT mein, har artifact create ya modify hone pe transport request ko assigned hota hai. QA testing ke baad, transport SE10 ke through released hota hai aur STMS ke through production mein imported hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: All RAP artifacts are transported together in a single transport request — the database table, draft table, CDS view entity, behavior definition, behavior implementation class, service definition, and service binding. The transport import sequence matters — the database table must exist before the CDS view is activated, and the CDS view must exist before the BDEF activates. In Eclipse ADT, each artifact is assigned to the transport request when created or modified. After QA testing, the transport is released via SE10 and imported to production via STMS.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: First QA transport mein draft table bhool gaye transport mein add karna. Production import ke baad draft features fail hone lage — CX_SADL exception. Transport correction note banaya — draft table added, re-imported. Lesson: always include draft table in transport checklist. Ab hamare project mein transport checklist document hai specifically for RAP artifacts.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Service binding transport karna bhoolna — table aur CDS transport ho gaye but service binding nahi, toh OData endpoint production mein nahi milega. Aur transport import ke baad service binding production mein manually publish karna pad sakta hai — check karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Transport Checklist for RAP Artifacts:
" 1. ZPURCHASE_REQUEST     (DB Table — SE11)
" 2. ZBUDGET_MASTER        (DB Table — SE11)
" 3. ZDRAFT_PURCHREQ3      (Draft Table — SE11)
" 4. ZI_PURCHASEREQUEST3   (CDS Root View — .ddls)
" 5. ZI_PURCHASEITEM3      (CDS Child View — .ddls)
" 6. ZBP_PURCHREQ3         (Behavior Definition — .bdef)
" 7. ZBP_PURCHREQ3         (Behavior Impl Class — ABAP)
" 8. ZSD_PURCHASEREQUEST3  (Service Definition — .srvd)
" 9. ZSB_PURCHREQ3_UI      (Service Binding — .srvb)
" 10. Launchpad Config      (via Launchpad Admin — separate)
" After import: publish service binding in production

followupAnswerBank.question: What performance considerations did you handle in the RAP app?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Performance multiple points pe address hui. Pehla, CDS view ko lean rakha gaya — sirf necessary fields selected, koi SELECT star nahi. Doosra, validation mein, original implementation mein har record ke budget code ke liye loop ke andar SELECT tha — classic N-plus-one problem. Ise refactor kiya saare budget codes pehle collect karne ke liye, saare limits ek SELECT mein FOR ALL ENTRIES ke saath fetch karne ke liye, aur fir results pe loop karne ke liye. Teesra, line items table mein RequestId pe ek index tha Fiori mein header se items tak fast navigation support karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Performance was addressed at multiple points. First, the CDS view was kept lean — only necessary fields selected, no SELECT star. Second, in the validation, the original implementation had a SELECT inside a loop for each record's budget code — the classic N-plus-one problem. This was refactored to collect all budget codes first, fetch all limits in one SELECT with FOR ALL ENTRIES, and then loop over results. Third, the line items table had an index on RequestId to support fast navigation from header to items in Fiori.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Performance issue in UAT: validation was slow for bulk create of 50 requests at once (an import scenario). Profile showed 50 SELECTs in loop for budget check. Refactored to collect all budget codes, one SELECT for all, SORT + READ TABLE binary search for lookup. Validation time dropped from 8 seconds to 0.3 seconds for 50 records.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SELECT inside LOOP in RAP validation — classic performance mistake. READ ENTITIES correct use hai transactional buffer se fetch karne ke liye, but external lookups (budget master, material master) should be batched outside the loop.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" WRONG: SELECT inside LOOP (N+1 problem)
LOOP AT lt_req INTO DATA(ls).
  SELECT SINGLE approved_limit FROM zbudget_master
    WHERE budget_code = @ls-BudgetCode INTO @lv_limit.
  " 50 requests = 50 SELECTs
ENDLOOP.

" CORRECT: Batch SELECT outside loop
DATA lt_budget_codes TYPE TABLE OF zbudget_master-budget_code.
lt_budget_codes = VALUE #( FOR r IN lt_req ( r-BudgetCode ) ).
SELECT budget_code, approved_limit
  FROM zbudget_master
  WHERE budget_code IN @lt_budget_codes
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_budgets).
SORT lt_budgets BY budget_code.
LOOP AT lt_req INTO DATA(ls).
  READ TABLE lt_budgets WITH KEY budget_code = ls-BudgetCode
    BINARY SEARCH INTO DATA(ls_budget).
  IF ls-Amount > ls_budget-approved_limit. " check here
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you handle number ranges for the RequestId in managed RAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: RequestId ke liye number ranges SAP ke number range framework use karke managed the. Maine SNRO transaction mein ZPURCHREQ_NR number range object banaya. BDEF mein, late numbering declared tha. Behavior implementation mein, adjust_numbers method implemented tha — ye RAP framework dwara commit time pe call hota hai. adjust_numbers ke andar, cl_numberrange_objects=>number_get next number fetch karne ke liye call hota hai, aur result temporary client key se real key se MAPPED collection mein mapped hota hai. Late numbering gaps prevent karta hai — abandoned drafts number range consume nahi karte.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Request ID number ranges were managed using SAP's number range framework. I created a number range object ZPURCHREQ_NR in SNRO transaction. In the BDEF, late numbering was declared. In the behavior implementation, the adjust_numbers method was implemented — this is called by the RAP framework at commit time. Inside adjust_numbers, cl_numberrange_objects=>number_get is called to fetch the next number, and the result is mapped from the temporary client key to the real key in the MAPPED collection. Late numbering prevents gaps — abandoned drafts do not consume number range.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Business requirement was strict: no gaps in PR numbers for audit compliance. Late numbering was the only correct approach — number is assigned only when user clicks Save, not when they open the create form. QA tested by opening 10 forms and abandoning 9 — only one PR number was assigned. Audit team approved the approach.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Early numbering use karna when no-gap requirement exists — early numbering assigns number on create, abandoned forms waste numbers. Aur MAPPED mein temp key to real key mapping skip karna adjust_numbers mein — subsequent item creates in same transaction will fail.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BDEF: declare late numbering
define behavior for ZI_PurchaseRequest3 ...
{ ...; late numbering; }

" Behavior Impl: adjust_numbers method
METHOD adjust_numbers.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(ls_entity).
    " Get next number from range
    cl_numberrange_objects=>number_get(
      EXPORTING
        nr_range_nr = '01'
        object      = 'ZPURCHREQ_NR'
      IMPORTING
        number      = DATA(lv_number) ).
    " Map temp key to real key
    APPEND VALUE #(
      %cid     = ls_entity-%cid
      %key-RequestId = lv_number ) TO mapped-purchreq.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you handle errors from external system calls in RAP actions?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Approve action mein, requester ko email alerts bhejne ke liye ek external notification API call included tha. Kyunki external calls fail ho sakti hain, action implementation ne HTTP call ko TRY-CATCH block mein wrap kiya. Exception pe, FAILED mein add karne ke bajaye (jo approve operation block karta), maine REPORTED mein ek warning-level entry add ki — user ko inform karte hue ki approval save hua lekin notification fail hua. Ek separate log table failure ko monitoring ke liye capture karti thi. Ye approach ensure karti hai ki core business operation complete ho chahe non-critical integration fail ho.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In the approve action, an external notification API call was included to send email alerts to the requester. Since external calls can fail, the action implementation wrapped the HTTP call in a TRY-CATCH block. On exception, instead of adding to FAILED (which would block the approve operation), I added a warning-level entry to REPORTED — informing the user that approval was saved but notification failed. A separate log table captured the failure for monitoring. This approach ensures the core business operation completes even if a non-critical integration fails.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production mein ek bar email server down tha. Without TRY-CATCH, every approve action would have failed completely — managers could not process 200 pending requests. With TRY-CATCH, approvals went through, warnings logged, IT team notified separately. Business continuity maintained. This design decision — separate critical from non-critical operations — is something I always document in technical design.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: External call failure ko FAILED collection mein daalna — yeh critical business operation (approve) ko bhi block kar deta hai for a non-critical side effect (notification). Har external call ke liye graceful degradation design karo — core operation chahiye succeed even if peripheral operations fail.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
METHOD approve FOR ACTION
    IMPORTING keys FOR PurchReq~approve RESULT et_result.
  " ... status update logic ...
  " Non-critical external notification
  LOOP AT lt_req INTO DATA(ls).
    TRY.
        " Send email notification via RFC/HTTP
        CALL FUNCTION 'Z_SEND_PR_NOTIFICATION'
          EXPORTING iv_request_id = ls-RequestId
                    iv_status     = 'APPROVED'.
      CATCH cx_sy_communication_error cx_rfc_not_found
            INTO DATA(lx_comm).
        " Warning only - do not add to FAILED
        APPEND VALUE #( %key = ls-%key
          %msg = new_message_with_text(
            severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-warning
            text = 'Approved. Notification failed - inform manually.' ) )
          TO reported-purchreq.
        " Log failure for monitoring
        INSERT znotif_error_log FROM VALUE #(
          request_id = ls-RequestId  log_time = sy-uzeit ).
    ENDTRY.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you implement search functionality in the RAP list report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Search functionality do levels pe implemented thi. Structured filtering Status, BudgetCode, aur Requester fields pe @UI.selectionField annotations use karti thi — ye Fiori list report filter bar mein filter controls ke roop mein appear hote hain, OData $filter parameters generate karte hue jo automatically CDS layer dwara handled hote hain. Free-text search Requester field pe @Search.defaultSearchElement use karta tha, Fiori search bar ko OData $search queries generate karne enable karte hue. CDS framework in annotations ke based pe actual database filtering handle karta hai — koi custom search code nahi chahiye tha.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Search functionality was implemented at two levels. Structured filtering used @UI.selectionField annotations on Status, BudgetCode, and Requester fields — these appear as filter controls in the Fiori list report filter bar, generating OData $filter parameters automatically handled by the CDS layer. Free-text search used @Search.defaultSearchElement on the Requester field, enabling the Fiori search bar to generate OData $search queries. The CDS framework handles the actual database filtering based on these annotations — no custom search code was needed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Finance team wanted to filter by budget code quickly. Added @UI.selectionField: [{position: 10}] on BudgetCode field. After ADT preview refresh — budget code filter appeared in filter bar automatically. Finance team could now filter to their budget codes directly. No backend change, no Fiori frontend change — just one annotation line.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: @UI.selectionField add karna lekin CDS view mein field properly exposed nahi karna — filter appears but crashes. Aur text search ke liye @Search annotation add karna lekin service definition mein search enable karna bhoolna — some versions require explicit search enablement in srvd file.

rap-bopf-022.interviewScriptHinglish: ek real S/4HANA implementation project mein, maine managed RAP use karke ek Purchase Request Fiori app develop ki. Business requirement thi paper-based PR process ko digitize karna budget validation aur manager approval ke saath. Maine PR header ke liye ek CDS root view entity aur line items ke liye ek child view entity design ki, composition association se linked. BDEF mein create, update aur delete operations define kiye, multi-step form filling ke liye draft handling, on save ek budget check validation, aur submit, approve aur reject actions manager workflow ke liye. Validation budget master table read karta tha aur requested amount approved limit se zyada hone par saves block karta tha, REPORTED through %element ke saath Fiori mein Amount field pe field-level error highlighting ke saath. Maine ek OData V4 UI service binding publish kiya aur Fiori Launchpad pe proper semantic object routing ke saath app configure kiya. Post go-live, maine /IWFND/ERROR_LOG aur /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG ke through issues monitor kiye. Maine jo key challenges solve kiye usme draft table key structure ko active table ke saath align karna aur missing budget master records ko gracefully handle karna shamil tha taki validation dump ki jagah ek clear message produce kare.

rap-bopf-023.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Production mein RAP ya BOPF related incident aaya — kya karte hain? Pehla rule: panic mat karo. Structured approach lo. Step 1: Symptoms note karo — kya error hai? Fiori app open nahi ho raha? Data save nahi ho raha? Specific action fail ho raha? Error message kya hai? Step 2: Error logs check karo. /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG transaction — yahan OData framework errors capture hote hain. Timestamp se recent errors find karo. Error message aur HTTP status code note karo. Step 3: Service binding check karo ADT mein — Published status hai ya nahi? Kabhi kabhi transport ke baad manually publish karna padta hai. Step 4: SICF check karo — /n/sicf transaction. OData V4 service nodes active hain ya nahi. Inactive service = 404 error Fiori pe. Step 5: Authorization check karo — SU53 transaction se check karo last failed authorization. Step 6: Application log check karo — SLG1 transaction mein. Step 7: Production pe directly debug karna usually allowed nahi hota — reproduce QA pe aur wahan debug karo. Yahi structured approach hai — real ABAP developer production incident mein yahi karta hai.
rap-bopf-023.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Production incident handling RAP/BOPF/Fiori ke liye — interview mein structured troubleshooting dikhana chahiye. Rote answer mat do — logical flow explain karo.

Incident Types aur Investigation Points:

Type 1: Fiori App Not Loading (HTTP 404 or blank screen)
- /SICF check: HTTP Handler active hai kya? Node /sap/opu/odata4 active hona chahiye.
- Service Binding published hai kya? ADT mein binding open karo — Published status check karo.
- BSP app active hai kya? /n/sicf mein BSP application node active check karo.
- Browser network tab: actual 404 URL note karo — service name se root cause milega.

Type 2: OData Errors / Save Failing
- /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG: OData framework errors yahan capture hote hain. Date/time filter lagao. Error detail mein HTTP status, OData error code, ABAP exception trace milega.
- SM50 / SM66: Work processes check karo — koi stuck hai kya? Deadlock?
- ST05: SQL trace karo performance issue ke liye.

Type 3: Authorization Errors
- SU53: Last failed authorization object check karo for affected user.
- SUIM: Kaunse roles user ke paas hain check karo.
- DCL (CDS access control) check karo — /IWBEP/ log mein AUTH_CHECK_FAILED type error hoga.

Type 4: Validation Not Working Correctly
- ADT BDEF check: Validation declared hai with correct trigger (on save vs on modify)?
- Behavior implementation class: Syntax error na ho — activation error hoga.
- REPORTED aur FAILED dono populated hain kya? Sirf ek hone pe partial behavior hota hai.

Type 5: Draft Not Working
- Draft table exist karti hai kya in production?
- Draft table transported tha kya transport mein?
- BDEF mein draft declaration hai kya?

Production Debug Rules:
- Production pe breakpoints usually not allowed (performance, security).
- Reproduce in QA/dev with same data.
- ABAP trace ST12 ya ABAP profiler use karo.
- /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG is your best friend — always first stop.
- Coordinate with Basis team for SICF, transport import verification.
- Fiori Launchpad issues: Launchpad admin check target mapping — semantic object aur action match karna hai.
rap-bopf-023.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'For a production incident involving RAP or Fiori OData, my first stop is /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on the gateway side and /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG on the backend — these capture OData framework errors with timestamps, HTTP status codes, and ABAP exception details. If the Fiori app is not loading, I check the service binding published status in ADT and verify the SICF node is active under /sap/opu/odata4. For authorization failures I run SU53 for the affected user to see the last failed authorization object, and cross-check DCL access control on the CDS view. If a validation is not triggering or the save is behaving unexpectedly, I check the BDEF activation status and verify both REPORTED and FAILED collections are populated together in the handler. For draft issues, I verify the draft table exists in production and was included in the transport. I never debug directly on production — I reproduce in QA with the same data, use ST05 for SQL trace when the symptom is performance, and SAT or ST12 for ABAP execution trace.'
rap-bopf-023.codeExamples:
" === Production Incident Diagnosis Checklist ===

" Step 1: Check OData Error Log
" Transaction: /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG
" Filter: Date = today, Time = incident window
" Look for: HTTP 4xx/5xx, OData error codes, ABAP exception
" Example error: CX_SADL_DRAFT_NOT_FOUND = draft table issue

" Step 2: Check Service Binding Status (ADT)
" ADT > Project Explorer > ZSB_PURCHREQ_UI (Service Binding)
" Status must show: Published
" If not published: Right-click > Publish
" After transport import, always re-publish in target system

" Step 3: Check SICF Active Status
" Transaction: /n/sicf
" Navigate: default_host > sap > opu > odata4
" Node must be Active (not greyed out)
" If inactive: Right-click > Activate Service
" Also check: sap/bc/ui5_ui5 for BSP app

" Step 4: Check Authorization for Affected User
" Transaction: SU53 (for the failing user's session)
" Shows last failed authorization object + field values
" Cross-check with CDS DCL: ZI_PURCHASEREQUEST3.dcl file
" DCL condition may be blocking due to missing PFCG role

" Step 5: SQL Trace for Performance Issues
" Transaction: ST05 (SQL Trace) or ST12 (ABAP Trace)
" Activate for affected user
" Reproduce the slow operation
" Deactivate trace, analyze results
" Look for: SELECT inside loops, missing indexes, large result sets

" Step 6: Check BDEF Activation in Development
" If validation not working in production after transport:
" ADT > Open BDEF > Check activation status
" Inactive BDEF = validation logic not deployed
" Activate in source, re-transport, re-import

If the Fiori app is not loading, I check the service binding published status in ADT and verify the SICF node is active under /sap/opu/odata4. commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Log mein exact error aur location milti hai — assumptions se zyada accurate.

rap-bopf-023.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Production incident playbook for RAP, BOPF and Fiori issues.

Used for:
- Triaging Fiori 4xx and 5xx errors against backend logs
- Verifying transport delivery in target system after import
- Diagnosing authorization and performance regressions

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG and /IWFND/ERROR_LOG for OData errors

followupAnswerBank.question: What does /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG show and how do you use it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Transaction /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG OData framework error log hai jo SAP system mein saare OData V4 service errors capture karta hai. Ye timestamp, user, service name, HTTP method, HTTP status code, OData error code, aur ABAP exception class aur message dikhata hai. Production incident mein, main date aur time se incident window tak filter karta hu aur relevant entries dhundhta hu. HTTP 404 service ya resource not found indicate karta hai — often ek service binding ya SICF issue. HTTP 500 ABAP exception trace ke saath behavior implementation mein backend code errors point karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Transaction /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG is the OData framework error log that captures all OData V4 service errors in the SAP system. It shows the timestamp, user, service name, HTTP method, HTTP status code, OData error code, and the ABAP exception class and message. In a production incident, I filter by date and time to the incident window and look for relevant entries. An HTTP 404 indicates service or resource not found — often a service binding or SICF issue. HTTP 500 with an ABAP exception trace points to backend code errors in the behavior implementation.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production pe users bolte the 'save nahi ho raha.' /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG check kiya — HTTP 500 with CX_SADL_VALIDATION_FAILED exception. Stack trace mein validateBudget method line number dikhi. QA pe reproduce kiya — pata chala budget master mein ek budget code ka record delete ho gaya tha by mistake. Restore kiya — issue resolved.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Production issue pe directly SE80 ya ADT mein code dekha aur assume kiya bug hai code mein — first always check error log. Log mein exact error aur location milti hai — assumptions se zyada accurate.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG Navigation:
" Transaction: /n/IWBEP/ERROR_LOG
" Date: today (incident date)
" Time From: 09:00:00 (incident time window start)
" Time To: 09:30:00
" Execute (F8)

" Error Entry Example:
" Date: 2025-06-01   Time: 09:14:33
" User: JOHN_SMITH   Service: ZSB_PURCHREQ_UI
" HTTP Method: POST  Status: 500
" OData Error: /IWBEP/CM_V4_SEC_RT/002
" Exception: CX_SADL_DRAFT_NOT_FOUND
" Detail: Draft table ZDRAFT_PURCHREQ3 does not exist

" Root cause found: draft table not transported
" Fix: Include ZDRAFT_PURCHREQ3 in transport, import to production

followupAnswerBank.question: What do you check when a RAP app returns HTTP 404?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: RAP Fiori app se HTTP 404 typically teen causes hote hain. Pehla, service binding target system mein published nahi hai — transport import ke baad, service binding ko manually ADT mein publish karna padta hai. Doosra, OData V4 ke liye SICF node inactive hai — /sicf check karo aur /sap/opu/odata4 aur uske subnodes activate karo. Teesra, Fiori app manifest mein URL actual published service binding path se match nahi karta — publishing ke baad ADT se exact URL copy karo aur manifest se compare karo. Browser developer tools network tab exact 404 URL dikhata hai jo mismatch identify karne mein help karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: HTTP 404 from a RAP Fiori app typically has three causes. First, the service binding is not published in the target system — after a transport import, the service binding must be published manually in ADT. Second, the SICF node for OData V4 is inactive — check /sicf and activate /sap/opu/odata4 and its subnodes. Third, the URL in the Fiori app manifest does not match the actual published service binding path — copy the exact URL from ADT after publishing and compare with manifest. Browser developer tools network tab shows the exact 404 URL which helps identify the mismatch.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Post-transport to production: users got 404 on app open. /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG mein entry nahi thi kyunki service itself wasn't found. Basis team se poochha — SICF mein odata4 node inactive tha in production. Activated it — 404 resolved. Second issue: manifest URL mein typo tha — zsb_purchreq_u instead of zsb_purchreq_ui. Fixed URL — app loaded. Two separate 404 causes, two separate fixes.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: 404 pe seedha code debug karne lagna — 404 usually infrastructure/configuration issue hai, not code bug. SICF aur service binding check first — ye 5 minute ka check hai aur 80% of 404s solve ho jaate hain.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" 404 Investigation Checklist:

" 1. Check Service Binding Published Status
" ADT > Project Explorer > ZSB_PURCHREQ_UI
" Look for: [Published] indicator in binding details
" If not published: Right-click > Publish Local Service Endpoint

" 2. Check SICF Node Active
" /n/sicf
" Path: default_host > sap > opu > odata4
" Right-click > Test Service — if 404, activate it
" Also: default_host > sap > bc > ui5_ui5 (for BSP app)

" 3. Verify URL in Fiori Manifest
" Correct pattern:
" /sap/opu/odata4/sap/zsb_purchreq_ui/srvd/sap/zsd_purchaserequest3/0001/
" Common mistake: missing trailing slash or wrong service name

" 4. Browser Developer Tools
" F12 > Network tab > filter 'odata4'
" Find the 404 request > copy exact URL
" Compare with expected URL from ADT service binding

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle an authorization-related production incident in RAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: RAP mein authorization-related incidents ke liye, pehla tool SU53 hai — ye user session ke liye last failed authorization check dikhata hai, including authorization object aur field values jo fail hue. Main affected user ko error encounter karne ke turant baad SU53 run karne aur screenshot share karne ko bolta hu. Fir main unke role assignments SU01 ya SUIM ke through check karta hu. CDS view DCL-based access control ke liye, /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG AUTH_CHECK_FAILED type entries dikhayega. Main CDS view ke DCL file ko examine karta hu samajhne ke liye kaunsi condition user ko block kar rahi hai aur user ke actual organizational data se compare karta hu.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For authorization-related incidents in RAP, the first tool is SU53 — it shows the last failed authorization check for a user session, including the authorization object and field values that failed. I ask the affected user to run SU53 immediately after encountering the error and share the screenshot. I then check their role assignments via SU01 or SUIM. For CDS view DCL-based access control, /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG will show AUTH_CHECK_FAILED type entries. I examine the DCL file for the CDS view to understand what condition is blocking the user and compare with the user's actual organizational data.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Finance user could not see purchase requests from a specific plant. SU53 showed no auth failure — it was not an authorization object issue. /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG showed DCL filter applying — WHERE plant = pfcg_auth. User's role did not include the new plant code that was recently added. PFCG role updated with new plant, transport sent — issue resolved in 2 hours.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SU53 check karna lekin yeh bhoolna ki CDS DCL row-level filtering ka SU53 mein koi entry nahi aata — DCL silently filters rows. Agar user bolega '0 records' (not 'no access error'), DCL row filter suspect karo, not authorization object.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Authorization Incident Toolkit:

" SU53: Shows last auth check failure
" Run as: affected user OR check their session
" Output: Auth object, field, value that failed

" SUIM: Find users with specific role or auth object
" Transaction: SUIM
" Users by Roles > enter Z_PURCHREQ_FINANCE > find users

" SU01: View user's role assignment directly
" Transaction: SU01 > enter user ID > Roles tab

" DCL Check: Who can see which data
" ADT > CDS View > ZI_PurchaseRequest3 > DCL tab
" Review condition:
" WHERE Requester = pfcg_auth(Z_PURCHREQ, REQUESTER, ACTVT='03')
" Finance role missing REQUESTER = '*' (all users) -> filtered out

" Fix: Update PFCG role Z_PURCHREQ_FINANCE
" Authorization Object Z_PURCHREQ
" Field REQUESTER: * (all values)
" Transport: SE10 > release > STMS import

followupAnswerBank.question: What do you do when a RAP validation stops working after a transport?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab transport ke baad validation kaam karna band kar de, main teen cheezein check karta hu. Pehla, ADT use karke production mein BDEF activation status — agar ye dependency ya syntax issue ki wajah se activate hone mein fail hua, validation deployed nahi hai. Doosra, main verify karta hu ki behavior implementation class transport mein included thi aur successfully imported hui — SE10 transport logs check karo. Teesra, main check karta hu ki CDS view production mein active hai — BDEF ispe depend karta hai. Koi bhi inactive dependency silently pure RAP object ko disable kar deti hai. Agar BDEF inactive hai, main change management process ke saath confirm karne ke baad production mein ADT ke through manually activate karta hu.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a validation stops working after a transport, I check three things. First, the BDEF activation status in production using ADT — if it failed to activate due to a dependency or syntax issue, the validation is not deployed. Second, I verify the behavior implementation class was included in the transport and imported successfully — check SE10 transport logs. Third, I check the CDS view is active in production — the BDEF depends on it. Any inactive dependency silently disables the whole RAP object. If the BDEF is inactive, I activate it manually in production via ADT after confirming with the change management process.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Post-transport: users could save records with zero amount — validateBudget clearly allowed it. ADT production se connect kiya — BDEF status 'Inactive'. Transport log check kiya — BDEF activation had failed due to CDS view being imported after BDEF (sequence issue). Manually activated CDS first, then BDEF — validation started working. Transport sequence checklist update kiya — CDS before BDEF always.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: BDEF activation failure silently ko notice nahi karna — BDEF inactive rehe toh no error at runtime, lekin no validations, no actions — business logic completely missing hoti hai. Post-transport always BDEF activation status check karo production mein.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Post-Transport Validation Checklist:

" 1. Check CDS View Active in Production
" ADT > Connect to Production System
" Open ZI_PurchaseRequest3.ddls
" Status bar: should show 'Active'
" If not: Select All > Activate (check change management process)

" 2. Check BDEF Active in Production
" ADT > Open ZBP_PurchReq3.bdef
" Status bar: should show 'Active'
" If not: Activate manually
" Note: Direct production activation requires access + CAB approval

" 3. Check Transport Log in SE10
" Transaction: SE10 > find transport > Display
" Look for: any red (failed) objects in import log
" Failed object during import = not deployed

" 4. Verify via Fiori
" Create test record with invalid amount
" Click Save — error message should appear
" If no error: validation not active -> go back to step 1

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you monitor RAP/OData performance in production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: RAP aur OData ke liye production performance monitoring kai tools use karta hai. Transaction /IWFND/MONI OData request monitoring deta hai — har request ka response time, payload size, aur error status dikhate hue. ST05 SQL trace ek operation ke dauran saari database queries capture karta hai, N-plus-one issues, missing indexes, ya unnecessary large result sets reveal karte hue. ST12 ABAP execution time method level pe profile karta hai, identify karte hue ki kaunse behavior implementation methods slow hain. Ongoing monitoring ke liye, main recommend karta hu HTTP 5xx errors ke liye /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG pe alerting set up karna users ke report karne se pehle issues catch karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Production performance monitoring for RAP and OData uses several tools. Transaction /IWFND/MONI provides OData request monitoring — showing each request's response time, payload size, and error status. ST05 SQL trace captures all database queries during an operation, revealing N-plus-one issues, missing indexes, or unnecessary large result sets. ST12 profiles ABAP execution time at method level, identifying which behavior implementation methods are slow. For ongoing monitoring, I recommend setting up alerting on /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG for HTTP 5xx errors to catch issues before users report them.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production mein list report slow ho gaya tha — 8 seconds to load. /IWFND/MONI mein OData GET request dekha — 7.8 seconds. ST05 SQL trace activate kiya for the request — 450+ SQL statements detected. Root cause: CDS view mein ek CASE expression ne full table scan karwaya kyunki index us column pe nahi tha. SE11 mein secondary index banaya — load time 1.2 seconds ho gaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Performance issue pe seedha code review karna bina trace ke — assumptions karna. ST05 SQL trace se exact SQL statements dikhte hain — bilkul accurate. Aur /IWFND/MONI ignore karna — yeh OData layer ka dedicated monitoring tool hai, miss mat karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Performance Monitoring Toolkit:

" 1. OData Request Monitor
" Transaction: /IWFND/MONI
" Filter: Service = ZSB_PURCHREQ_UI, Date = today
" Look for: slow requests (>3 sec response time)
" Click request > View Payload to see data volume

" 2. SQL Trace
" Transaction: ST05 (SQL Trace)
" Activate for user: JOHN_SMITH
" Perform slow operation in Fiori
" ST05 > Deactivate > Analyze
" Look for: repeated same SQL = N+1, large row counts

" 3. ABAP Trace
" Transaction: ST12
" Set trace parameters for affected user/request
" Method-level time breakdown shown
" Identify: validateBudget 4.2 sec (SELECT in loop!)

" 4. Check Missing Index (SE11)
" Table: ZPURCHASE_REQUEST
" Create secondary index on BUDGET_CODE if missing
" After index creation: performance improves automatically

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a production incident where draft data is corrupted?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Production mein draft data corruption CX_SADL_DRAFT_CONFLICT jaisi errors ke roop mein ya user ke draft open ya save na kar paane ke roop mein manifest hota hai. Pehle, main /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG exact exception ke liye check karta hu. Fir main draft table ko directly SE16 ke through examine karta hu orphaned ya inconsistent draft records identify karne ke liye. Common causes mein same user dwara do browser sessions se concurrent edits, ya ek transport jo active table structure update kiya draft table update kiye bina shaamil hain. Individual records ke liye quickest resolution users ko Fiori mein Discard Draft action use karne ko bolna hai — jo RAP framework ke through draft cleanly clear karta hai. Structural mismatches ke liye, draft table ko active table structure ke saath align karna pad sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Draft data corruption in production manifests as errors like CX_SADL_DRAFT_CONFLICT or the user being unable to open or save a draft. First, I check /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG for the exact exception. Then I examine the draft table directly via SE16 to identify orphaned or inconsistent draft records. Common causes include concurrent edits by the same user from two browser sessions, or a transport that updated the active table structure without updating the draft table. The quickest resolution for individual records is asking users to use the Discard Draft action in Fiori — which clears the draft via the RAP framework cleanly. For structural mismatches, the draft table may need to be aligned with the active table structure.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production incident: 3 users could not open their saved drafts — error 'Draft not found'. Draft table check kiya — records wahan the but IsActiveEntity field inconsistent tha. It turned out a direct DB update (SE16N edit, not recommended) had been done on the active table bypassing RAP — this created inconsistency. Corrupt drafts deleted from draft table, users asked to recreate drafts — resolved. Root cause: direct DB edit bypass policy added to project guidelines.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Direct DB edit (SE16N) on RAP-managed active table karna — yeh RAP framework aur draft state ko corrupt karta hai. RAP-managed tables hamesha RAP framework ke through update karo — direct DB manipulation bypass karta hai consistency checks.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Draft Incident Investigation:

" 1. Check Error Log
" /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG > search: CX_SADL_DRAFT

" 2. Examine Draft Table directly
" Transaction: SE16N
" Table Name: ZDRAFT_PURCHREQ3
" Execute > check entries for affected user
" Columns: REQUEST_ID, IS_ACTIVE_ENTITY, HAS_DRAFT_ENTITY, ...

" 3. Discard Draft via OData (clean approach)
" POST /sap/opu/odata4/.../PurchReq(RequestId='0000000042',
"      IsActiveEntity=false)/com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1.DiscardDraft
" This clears draft table entry properly via RAP framework

" 4. If batch cleanup needed (many orphaned drafts)
" Only with proper authorization and change management:
" DELETE FROM zdraft_purchreq3
"   WHERE request_id IN @lt_corrupt_ids
"   AND is_active_entity = abap_false.

followupAnswerBank.question: What logs do you check for a Fiori Launchpad tile not loading?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab Fiori Launchpad tile load nahi hota, investigation RAP code ke bajaye infrastructure aur configuration ko span karti hai. Browser developer tools (F12) Network aur Console tabs pehla stop hain — ye exact failing HTTP request aur JavaScript errors dikhate hain. Launchpad admin tool dikhata hai ki target mapping configuration correct hai ya nahi — semantic object aur action exactly app router configuration se match hone chahiye. SICF mein BSP application node active hona chahiye. Agar app load hone ke baad OData call khud fail karta hai, toh investigation service binding aur /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG pe shift hoti hai. Main distinguish karta hu 'tile appear nahi hota' (Launchpad config) aur 'tile appear hota hai lekin app crash hota hai' (app ya OData issue) ke beech.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a Fiori Launchpad tile does not load, the investigation spans infrastructure and configuration rather than RAP code. Browser developer tools (F12) Network and Console tabs are the first stop — they show the exact failing HTTP request and JavaScript errors. The Launchpad admin tool shows if the target mapping configuration is correct — semantic object and action must exactly match the app router configuration. SICF must have the BSP application node active. If the OData call itself fails after the app loads, then investigation shifts to service binding and /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG. I distinguish between 'tile does not appear' (Launchpad config) and 'tile appears but app crashes' (app or OData issue).
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: User: 'tile nahi dikh raha.' Launchpad admin check kiya — target mapping tha, tile tha — but role restriction on tile. User ka role Z_PURCHREQ_USER tha, tile was assigned to Z_PURCHREQ_MANAGER only. Role updated for user — tile appeared. Entire investigation: 15 minutes. Root cause: role assignment, not code.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Launchpad tile issue pe ABAP code debugging start karna — code tak pahunche hi nahi the, Launchpad config issue tha. Always front-to-back debug: browser → Launchpad → SICF → OData → RAP code. Order matter karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Launchpad Tile Not Loading — Diagnosis Steps:

" 1. Browser F12 > Network Tab
" Filter: fetch/xhr
" Look for: Red (failed) requests
" Status 404: service not found or SICF inactive
" Status 403: authorization issue
" Status 200 with empty response: data issue

" 2. Browser F12 > Console Tab
" Look for: JavaScript errors, uncaught exceptions
" Common: 'Cannot read property of undefined' = missing annotation

" 3. Launchpad Admin Tool
" Transaction: /n/ui2/flpd_cust
" Check: Target Mapping > Semantic Object + Action
" Must match: app's manifest 'crossNavigation > outbounds' config

" 4. SICF Active Check
" /n/sicf
" Path: default_host > sap > bc > ui5_ui5 > <app BSP name>
" Must show: Active (green)
" If inactive: right-click > Activate Service

" 5. Role/Authorization for Tile Visibility
" Launchpad tiles can be role-restricted
" PFCG: check if tile group assigned to user's role

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you escalate a production incident that you cannot resolve?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab production incident independently resolve nahi ho sakta, main structured escalation process follow karta hu. Main /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG se exact error compile karta hu including exception class aur stack trace, reproduce karne ke steps, affected users ki number, aur business impact severity. Agar ye SAP framework issue lagta hai — for example custom code se related na hone wala OData V4 framework exception — main SAP ke saath OSS ticket open karta hu aur error log attach karta hu. Infrastructure issues jaise SICF ya system performance ke liye, main Basis team ko involve karta hu. BOPF ya RAP-specific complex issues ke liye, main topic pe SAP Notes check karta hu. Possible ho toh main workaround bhi establish karta hu — for example, Fiori issue investigate hone ke dauran temporary bypass ke roop mein direct SAPGUI access.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a production incident cannot be resolved independently, I follow a structured escalation process. I compile the exact error from /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG including the exception class and stack trace, the steps to reproduce, the number of users affected, and the business impact severity. If it appears to be a SAP framework issue — for example an OData V4 framework exception not related to custom code — I open an OSS ticket with SAP and attach the error log. For infrastructure issues like SICF or system performance, I involve the Basis team. For BOPF or RAP-specific complex issues, I check SAP Notes on the topic. I also establish a workaround if possible — for example, direct SAPGUI access as a temporary bypass while the Fiori issue is investigated.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: One production incident: CX_SADL_MAPPING_TRANSFORM error in OData V4 after a system upgrade. Error only appeared for date fields in German locale. Neither my debugging nor Basis found a fix. OSS ticket opened with error log attached. SAP responded with a Note — a known bug in that patch level for date locale handling in SADL. Note applied — issue resolved. Without escalation, we would have wasted days. Knowing when to escalate is as important as knowing how to debug.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Escalation se darna — yeh weakness nahi hai. Sahi time pe sahi person ko involve karna professional maturity hai. Aur escalation mein vague information dena: 'something is broken' — specific information dena: 'HTTP 500 with CX_SADL_MAPPING_TRANSFORM in validateBudget method after system upgrade to 2024 SP03.' Specific info = faster resolution.

rap-bopf-023.interviewScriptHinglish: RAP ya Fiori OData wali production incident ke liye, mera first stop hai gateway side pe /IWFND/ERROR_LOG aur backend side pe /IWBEP/ERROR_LOG - ye OData framework errors timestamps, HTTP status codes aur ABAP exception details ke saath capture karte hain. Agar Fiori app load nahi ho rahi, main ADT mein service binding ka published status check karta hoon aur verify karta hoon ki SICF node /sap/opu/odata4 ke neeche active hai. Authorization failures ke liye main affected user ke liye SU53 run karta hoon ki last failed authorization object dekh sakoon, aur CDS view pe DCL access control cross-check karta hoon. Agar koi validation trigger nahi ho rahi ya save unexpected behave kar raha hai, main BDEF activation status check karta hoon aur verify karta hoon ki REPORTED aur FAILED collections dono handler mein saath populate ho rahe hain. Draft issues ke liye, verify karta hoon ki draft table production mein exist karta hai aur transport mein included tha. Main kabhi direct production pe debug nahi karta - QA mein same data ke saath reproduce karta hoon, performance symptom hone par SQL trace ke liye ST05 use karta hoon, aur ABAP execution trace ke liye SAT ya ST12.
